topic: Option 1: Relationships / Gender / Cognition and Development
subject: psychology

items.q: According to Maslow, when can self-actualisation be achieved?
items.correct: Only after lower deficiency needs are largely met.
items.misconception: Anyone can self-actualise at any time through willpower.
items.why: Students miss the hierarchical structure. You cannot pursue growth needs while physiological, safety, love, and esteem needs are unmet - that's the whole point of a hierarchy.

items.correct: Learning by observing the consequences others receive for behaviour.
items.misconception: Being directly rewarded for copying someone.
items.why: Students confuse vicarious with direct reinforcement. 'Vicarious' means experienced through someone else - you see them rewarded/punished and adjust your own behaviour accordingly.

items.q: Which component handles visual and spatial information?
items.why: Students mix up the slave systems. The phonological loop is for auditory/verbal information; the sketchpad is for visual/spatial. The central executive coordinates but doesn't store.

items.q: The SRY gene that triggers testis development sits on which chromosome?
items.why: It's Y. “13” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Kohlberg's final stage of gender development is:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Gender constancy. “Gender identity” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: At roughly what age does Kohlberg say gender constancy is reached?
items.why: Exact answer: 7 years. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
